The first real test of Hormuz normalization has arrived.

The IRGC turned back tankers on the southern route, signaling that Iran—not the market—sets the rules of transit.

Hormuz is open. Confidence isn't. #OOTT #Oil #Hormuz #Shipping #IranWar‌
quoted_text:
A week of widening commercial confidence in the Strait of Hormuz has hit its first significant test. 

On June 25, the IRGC ordered five tankers to turn back along the southern route, warning that unauthorized transits occur at their own risk. By targeting a lane operators viewed as unrestricted, Iran appears to be extending its oversight across the corridors. 

While Hormuz remains operationally open with 43 transits recorded after the incident, the pace of normalization has slowed. https://t.c

quoted_text:
Strait of Hormuz crossings surge as operators shift to Omani route

Confirmed vessel activity through the Strait of Hormuz rose sharply on 24 June, with 70 crossings recorded, up 105% day-on-day, as demining efforts advanced and more operators used the Omani route.

According to #MarineTraffic data, commercial traffic accounted for most of the activity, with 53 transits, while low-risk vessels dominated the day’s profile. The increase suggests a short-term confidence boost following the US-Iran

quoted_text:
Brent just flipped into contango. Almost nobody saw it coming.

Hormuz is reopening, AG crude is flooding back, and the market is extrapolating a supply wave forward. But is the crisis really over?

Inside this episode, @NGCanalyst, @JuneGoh_Sparta, and Abhishek Kumar break down:

▸ Brent in contango – buying opportunity or fair value?
▸ The "Asian crude buffet" – PG glut, Iranian waiver, no China
▸ Refinery runrates: Max is what we need, but can the units deliver?

Listen now: https://t.co/SmsD
